ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_traffic" (
  "traffic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network communication */,
  "time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the communication */,
  "proto" INTEGER /* ID of the network protocol used */,
  "sip" INTEGER /* ID of the source IP address */,
  "sport" INTEGER /* ID of the source port number */,
  "dip" INTEGER /* ID of the destination IP address */,
  "dport" INTEGER /* ID of the destination port number */,
  "from_email" TEXT /* Email address of the sender */,
  "to_email" TEXT /* Email address of the recipient */,
  "subject" TEXT /* Subject line of the email */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the email (if available) */,
  "traffic_size" INTEGER /* Size of the network traffic in bytes */,
  "traffic_type" TEXT /* Type of traffic (e.g., internal, external, incoming, outgoing) */,
  "is_encrypted" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the traffic is encrypted */,
  "encryption_type" TEXT /* Type of encryption used (e.g., TLS, SSL) */,
  "payload_hash" TEXT /* Hash of the payload for integrity checks */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device generating the traffic */,
  "geo_location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the source or destination */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for HTTP traffic */,
  "status_code" INTEGER /* Status code for HTTP traffic */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* ID of any associated security alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("traffic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_to_email FOREIGN KEY ("to_email") REFERENCES email_recipients ("email_address"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_dport FOREIGN KEY ("dport") REFERENCES ports ("port_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_content FOREIGN KEY ("content") REFERENCES email_content ("content_text"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_alert_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_id") REFERENCES security_alerts ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_sip FOREIGN KEY ("sip") REFERENCES ip_addresses ("ip_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_from_email FOREIGN KEY ("from_email") REFERENCES email_senders ("email_address"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_dip FOREIGN KEY ("dip") REFERENCES ip_addresses ("ip_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_subject FOREIGN KEY ("subject") REFERENCES email_subjects ("subject_text"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_sport FOREIGN KEY ("sport") REFERENCES ports ("port_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_proto FOREIGN KEY ("proto") REFERENCES protocols ("proto_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, user) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hash of the user's password for security */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the user account is active */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was created */,
  "modified_date" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was last modified */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* Last known IP address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ip_addresses" (
  "ip_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each IP address */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the IP address */,
  "registered_to" INTEGER /* ID of the user or department registered to the IP address */,
  "is_external" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the IP address is external */,
  "geo_location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the IP address */,
  "last_seen_date" TEXT /* Date and time the IP address was last seen */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ip_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ports" (
  "port_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each port */,
  "port_number" INTEGER /* Port number */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the port */,
  "is_common" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the port is commonly used */,
  "last_seen_date" TEXT /* Date and time the port was last seen */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("port_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "protocols" (
  "proto_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each protocol */,
  "proto_name" TEXT /* Name of the protocol (e.g., SMTP, HTTP) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the protocol */,
  "is_secure" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the protocol is secure */,
  "last_seen_date" TEXT /* Date and time the protocol was last seen */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("proto_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_subjects" (
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each email subject */,
  "subject_text" TEXT /* Text of the email subject */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the email subject (optional) */,
  "is_spam" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the subject is likely spam */,
  "spam_score" REAL /* Spam score of the subject */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_content" (
  "content_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each email content */,
  "content_text" TEXT /* Text of the email content */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the email content (optional) */,
  "is_spam" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the content is likely spam */,
  "spam_score" REAL /* Spam score of the content */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("content_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_attachments" (
  "attachment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each email attachment */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the attachment file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the attachment file in MB */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the attachment file (e.g., PDF, DOCX) */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "is_malicious" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the attachment is potentially malicious */,
  "malware_score" REAL /* Malware score of the attachment */,
  "hash" TEXT /* Hash of the attachment for integrity checks */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attachment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "traffic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network communication */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the log entry */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Message or event logged */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the log (e.g., info, warning, error) */,
  "log_source" TEXT /* Source of the log (e.g., firewall, router) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_logs_traffic_id FOREIGN KEY ("traffic_id") REFERENCES network_traffic ("traffic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "security_alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each security alert */,
  "traffic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network communication */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of the alert (e.g., phishing, data exfiltration) */,
  "alert_description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolved_date" TEXT /* Date and time the alert was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "alert_source" TEXT /* Source of the alert (e.g., firewall, IDS) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_alerts_traffic_id FOREIGN KEY ("traffic_id") REFERENCES network_traffic ("traffic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "traffic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network communication */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the access occurred */,
  "access_device" TEXT /* Device used for access (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_traffic_id FOREIGN KEY ("traffic_id") REFERENCES network_traffic ("traffic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_recipients" (
  "recipient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each recipient */,
  "email_address" TEXT /* Email address of the recipient */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user if the recipient is a registered user */,
  "is_registered" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the recipient is a registered user */,
  "is_internal" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the recipient is internal to the organization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("recipient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_recipients_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_senders" (
  "sender_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sender */,
  "email_address" TEXT /* Email address of the sender */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user if the sender is a registered user */,
  "is_registered" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the sender is a registered user */,
  "is_internal" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the sender is internal to the organization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sender_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_senders_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
